title: Austin Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet Reaches 10 Model Y Vehicles
summary: Tesla's Austin Robotaxi fleet has hit 10 confirmed unsupervised Model Y vehicles, the highest count yet for driverless service in the city.
impact_reason: Hitting 10 unsupervised vehicles in Austin shows incremental growth in the core fleet, mildly boosting scaling velocity toward unsupervised operations.

title: Tesla Plans to Double Austin Robotaxi Fleet to ~60 by December
summary: Elon Musk announced plans to double the Austin Robotaxi fleet from 29-30 current vehicles to about 60 next month, far short of the prior 500-vehicle end-2025 target. All current Austin rides remain supervised with safety drivers.
impact_reason: Promises fleet growth but remains supervised and well below targets, resulting in neutral net effect on unsupervised scaling velocity in Austin.

title: Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach Four Vehicles Total After Launch
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows two unsupervised vehicles active in each of Dallas and Houston shortly after launch, compared to 13 unsupervised in Austin over the last 30 days. This is part of a planned seven-city expansion in H1 2026.
impact_reason: Early fleet additions in new cities indicate initial scaling progress, contributing positively to unsupervised operations velocity in Texas beyond Austin.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla announced the rollout of unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston, Texas, with initial geofence areas and video evidence of no safety drivers. This triples Tesla's active Robotaxi hubs in Texas, building on ongoing operations in Austin.
impact_reason: Expanding to two additional Texas cities demonstrates rapid geographic scaling of unsupervised operations, enabling faster data collection and fleet growth across the state despite small initial deployments.

title: Tesla Deploys Minimal Fleet in Dallas and Houston Launches
summary: Crowdsourced tracking data shows Tesla deployed only one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch, expanding to two vehicles per city by Tuesday morning, compared to 13 active unsupervised vehicles in Austin. The Bay Area fleet remains significantly larger at over 500 vehicles.
impact_reason: While geographically significant, the extremely limited vehicle deployment (2 per city) reveals slow absolute scaling velocity and suggests Austin remains the primary unsupervised testing ground, making this a neutral operational reality check rather than a meaningful acceleration catalyst.

title: Austin Robotaxis Involved in 14 Crashes Since Launch
summary: A February 2026 filing disclosed 14 crashes involving Austin robotaxis since service launch.
impact_reason: Disclosing 14 crashes highlights safety risks that could prompt regulatory scrutiny or slower scaling of unsupervised fleet in Austin.
